mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.4 percentage points within a single month, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor demand that triggers recession fears, compressing equity multiples and driving negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the predictive power of UNRATE jumps vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of industrial production and housing starts indicates a synchronized demand-supply shock that erodes corporate profit margins, leading to negative equity returns over the following month as earnings revisions turn downward.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO decline × HOUST decline) shows no incremental predictive power over a multivariate regression model with R-squared improvement < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPI exceeds 3.5%, subsequent increases in the federal funds rate fail to anchor inflation expectations, causing real rates to spike and equity valuations to contract over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not decline when both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS rise simultaneously, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP expands the earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, generating positive forward returns over a 60-day horizon as cash flow expectations are revised upward and dividend growth accelerates.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 growth remains negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no correlation with GDP growth surprises.

mechanism_narrative: When total nonfarm payrolls exceed 200,000 for two consecutive months, it confirms robust labor market tightness that supports consumer spending and drives positive equity returns over the next week as near-term revenue visibility improves.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns in > 55% of out-of-sample instances, or the effect disappears when controlling for concurrent FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in financial stress (proxied by RSAFS decline) and unemployment indicates tightening credit conditions that restrict corporate investment and consumer borrowing, depressing equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal shows no incremental predictive power over a baseline model, or forward returns are not significantly lower when both RSAFS falls and UNRATE rises.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of supply chain normalization where industrial production rises while CPI remains sticky above 3.0%, margin compression from input cost pass-through delays reduces equity returns over the subsequent quarter as pricing power fades.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 0.5% MoM while CPIAUCSL > 3.0% YoY
failure_condition: Equity returns do not decline when INDPRO and CPIAUCSL diverge as specified, or the predictive relationship reverses sign in walk-forward tests spanning 2020-2023.
